**AFI's 100 Years...100 Movies – 10th Anniversary Edition** was the 2007 updated version of [AFI's 100 Years...100 Movies](/wiki/AFI%27s_100_Years...100_Movies "AFI's 100 Years...100 Movies"). The original list was unveiled in 1998.

## Criteria

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=AFI%27s_100_Years...100_Movies_(10th_Anniversary_Edition)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Criteria")]

AFI asked jurors to consider the following criteria in their selection process:

* [Feature length](/wiki/Feature_length "Feature length"): Narrative format typically over 60 minutes long.
* [American film](/wiki/Cinema_of_the_United_States "Cinema of the United States"): English language, with significant creative and/or financial production from the United States. (A number of films on the list were British-made but financed by American studios; these include *[Lawrence of Arabia](/wiki/Lawrence_of_Arabia_(film) "Lawrence of Arabia (film)")*, *[The Bridge on the River Kwai](/wiki/The_Bridge_on_the_River_Kwai "The Bridge on the River Kwai")*, and *[A Clockwork Orange](/wiki/A_Clockwork_Orange_(film) "A Clockwork Orange (film)")*.)
* [Critical recognition](/wiki/Film_criticism "Film criticism"): Formal commendation in print, television, and digital media.
* [Major award winner](/wiki/List_of_film_awards "List of film awards"): Recognition from competitive events including awards from peer groups, critics, guilds, and major film festivals.
* [Popularity over time](/wiki/Category:Lists_of_box_office_number-one_films_in_the_United_States "Category:Lists of box office number-one films in the United States"): Includes success at the box office, television and cable airings, and DVD/VHS sales and rentals.
* Historical significance: A film's mark on the history of the moving image through visionary narrative devices, technical innovation or other groundbreaking achievements.
* Cultural impact: A film's mark on American society in matters of style and substance.

* Of the 77 films that remained on the list, 36 improved their ranking, 38 saw their ranking decline, and three kept their positions: *Citizen Kane*, *The Godfather Part II*, and *The Best Years of Our Lives*.
* *[The Searchers](/wiki/The_Searchers "The Searchers")* had the highest increase in ranking, moving from #96 to #12. The greatest decrease without being dropped was *The African Queen*, which went from #17 to #65.
* The oldest film to be dropped was [D.W. Griffith](/wiki/D._W._Griffith "D. W. Griffith")'s *[The Birth of a Nation](/wiki/The_Birth_of_a_Nation "The Birth of a Nation")* (1915), from #44. The oldest film to be added was Griffith's *Intolerance* (1916) (#49).
* The newest film removed is *Fargo* (1996), the newest added *The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring* (2001), which is also the only film on the list released after 1999.
* The highest-ranked addition was *The General* at #18. The highest-ranked removal was *Doctor Zhivago* (#39).
* *Duck Soup*, featuring the [Marx Brothers](/wiki/Marx_Brothers "Marx Brothers"), moved up 25 positions to #60. It was replaced at #85 by another film starring them, *A Night at the Opera*.
* Seventy-three of the films were nominated for the [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards") for [Best Picture](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Picture "Academy Award for Best Picture"). Twenty-eight won, including *Sunrise* (1927), which won the [Academy Award for Unique and Artistic Production](/wiki/1st_Academy_Awards "1st Academy Awards") (an award that was only presented at the first ceremony). The original list has 75 Academy Awards Best Picture nominees and 33 winners.
* In the 2007 list, eight of the top ten films were nominated for the Academy Award for Best Picture, with five winning. In the original list, nine out of the top ten were nominees, and six won.
* Two animated films appear on each list. In 1998, *Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs* ranked at #49, and *Fantasia* at #58. *Snow White* moved up to #34 in 2007, while *Fantasia* was dropped, and *Toy Story* was added at #99. Three were produced by [Walt Disney Productions](/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company"), while *Toy Story* was a joint production of Walt Disney Studios and Pixar, making *Toy Story* the only [Pixar](/wiki/Pixar "Pixar") film on either list.

## Broadcast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=AFI%27s_100_Years...100_Movies_(10th_Anniversary_Edition)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Broadcast")]

This installment of the [American Film Institute](/wiki/American_Film_Institute "American Film Institute")'s (AFI) [Emmy Award](/wiki/Emmy_Award "Emmy Award")-winning [AFI 100 Years... series](/wiki/AFI_100_Years..._series "AFI 100 Years... series") counted down the movies in a three-hour television event airing on June 20, 2007, on [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS"). It was hosted by [Academy Award](/wiki/Academy_Award "Academy Award")-winning actor [Morgan Freeman](/wiki/Morgan_Freeman "Morgan Freeman"). The program considered classic favorites and newly eligible films released from 1997 to 2005.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The special was nominated for two [Emmy Awards](/wiki/Emmy_Awards "Emmy Awards").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
